steps.title: Identify the angle and its opposite side
steps.content: Angle $x°$ is at the bottom-right corner of the right triangle. For this angle, the opposite side is the vertical leg with length 28, and the hypotenuse is labeled $c$.

steps.title: Apply the sine ratio and extract k
steps.content:
The sine of an angle in a right triangle equals opposite divided by hypotenuse: $$\sin(x°) = \frac{28}{c}$$
We're given that $\sin(x°) = \frac{k}{c}$. Comparing these expressions directly, we get $k = 28$. The answer is 28.
